page: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance featuring an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa processor. It is configured with 2 shared vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The processor operates at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz and includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache. Storage is bundled as a 480 GB local NVMe SSD, and the instance includes 1 complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities but moderate multi-core and memory performance. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance ranks in the top tier, while multi-core performance falls into the average category. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression show strong results in the top 25 percent. Conversely, memory bandwidth for small blocks is weak, though large block read and write bandwidths maintain average speeds. Passmark CPU, database operations, and Redis SET operations benchmarks place the server in the bottom 25 percent, and static web serving throughput is poor, ranking in the bottom 10 percent. Large-model LLM inference is also limited, with 7B model text generation performing in the bottom 10 percent.

This instance offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 1 to 8, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient option for workloads requiring substantial memory relative to compute power, such as caching layers or lightweight databases. However, because the vCPUs are shared, users face potential performance variability under sustained multi-threaded loads, as reflected in the lower multi-core and database benchmark tiers. The server is best suited for memory-intensive but compute-moderate workloads, including development environments, small-scale caching, single-threaded data processing, and basic memory-optimized applications.
description: Vultr voc-s-2c-16gb-480s-amd is an Optimized Cloud Compute server instance configured with 2 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 16.0 GB of RAM, and a 480 GB local NVMe SSD. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz, this server features a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core execution and memory bandwidth for small blocks remain average to weak. Database operations, Redis SET performance, and static web serving throughput also rank in the lower percentiles. Due to its shared CPU allocation and high memory density, this instance represents a qualitatively cost-effective choice for memory-heavy but compute-moderate tasks. It is best suited for lightweight databases, caching layers, single-threaded processing, and development environments.
